title: Release Identity single source of truth and atomic release

sources.reason: Repository release contract requires source-bound evidence and immutable release handling.
Current release workflow creates a public release before post-release smoke verification.
Current distribution checker validates local tag files but does not verify release asset subject identity.
Current source-bound evidence generator defines the release asset evidence contract.
The current hard line ceiling must account for the authorized release identity verification increment until WI-09 replaces it with real complexity metrics.

problemStatement: v0.5.28 contains tag-local provenance for an older commit while final Release Assets attest a different commit, and post-release Smoke is asynchronous on main. The release path therefore has internally consistent but non-unique publication facts.

riskAssessment.reason: This changes release workflow and distribution verification, so a source-identity regression could publish a misleading release.

executionDecision.reason: The approved review defines the source identity, release asset, and draft-release acceptance boundaries.

preReviewWarnings: Review focus: ensure the workflow never publishes a release before verification and never accepts provenance whose subject differs from the immutable tag target.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Release Assets are generated from one exact immutable source SHA and their provenance subject, digest metadata, release tag, and tag target are cross-checked.
Git Tag content contains no candidate provenance that can be mistaken for final release evidence; Release Assets are the sole authoritative publication evidence.
The GitHub Release is created as Draft, exact-tag installation and evidence verification run before publication, and any failed verification prevents publication.
Regression tests cover mismatched tag target, mismatched provenance subject, digest mismatch, draft release sequencing, and exact-ref post-release verification.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: The published v0.5.28 tag and final release assets attest different source commits, so consumers cannot identify one immutable release fact.
constraints: Keep Release Assets source-bound to the exact requested SHA.
Do not treat committed candidate provenance as final publication evidence.
Do not publish a release until exact-tag verification passes.
intent.rationale: A single immutable release identity is required for calibrated trust in release evidence.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Release workflow publishes only after exact-tag evidence verification
scenarioCoverage.reason: Verify with workflow tests and a controlled release dry run.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Distribution checker rejects a provenance subject that differs from the tag target
Public release assets remain the sole authoritative release evidence
scenarioCoverage.reason: Verify generated asset schema and release workflow behavior.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly approved automated execution of the complete review remediation plan.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
